name: @ever-works/github-storage-plugin
description: GitHub-blob storage backend for Ever Works — stores uploaded objects as files in a configured repository or the Work's data repo. Supports Git LFS.

plugin.name: GitHub Storage
plugin.description: Stores uploaded objects as commits in a GitHub repository. Pick between a dedicated storage repo or reusing the Work's existing data repo, with optional Git LFS support.

envVars.name: GITHUB_STORAGE_MODE
envVars.description: `separate-repo` (default) or `data-repo`. `data-repo` reuses each Work's existing data repo and looks up coordinates per upload.

envVars.name: GITHUB_STORAGE_TOKEN
envVars.description: GitHub Personal Access Token with `contents:write` scope on the storage repo. Required in `separate-repo` mode; ignored in `data-repo` mode (the Work-owner OAuth token is used).

envVars.name: GITHUB_STORAGE_OWNER
envVars.description: Owner (user or org) of the storage repository. Required in `separate-repo` mode.

envVars.name: GITHUB_STORAGE_REPO
envVars.description: Storage repository name. Required in `separate-repo` mode.

envVars.name: GITHUB_STORAGE_PATH_PREFIX
envVars.description: Prefix prepended to every blob path (default `uploads`).

envVars.name: GITHUB_STORAGE_LFS_ENABLED
envVars.description: When true (default for fresh installs), uploads are stored as Git LFS objects with a pointer file in the tree. Defaults to false for already-deployed installs (set legacy env vars only, no mode set) to preserve byte-for-byte commit shape.

envVars.name: GITHUB_STORAGE_TRANSPORT
envVars.description: `auto` (default — picks per mode), `contents-api`, or `clone-and-push`. Controls how the pointer or blob is committed.

envVars.name: GITHUB_STORAGE_API_HOST
envVars.description: LFS Batch API host base. Defaults to `https://github.com`. GitHub Enterprise Server deployments set this to their host (e.g. `https://ghe.example.com`).

envVars.name: GITHUB_STORAGE_DATA_REPO_BRANCH
envVars.description: Branch the `data-repo` mode resolver uses for every Work's data repo. Defaults to `main`. Set to `master` (or a custom branch) for repos that don't follow the modern default.
